meta.title: Vais - AI-Optimized Systems Programming Language
meta.description: Vais is a token-efficient systems programming language designed for AI code generation with native LLVM performance.

hero.title:
Write fewer tokens,
get native performance
hero.subtitle: Vais (Vibe AI Language for Systems) is a systems programming language optimized for AI code generation. The current public baseline is a certified Core compiler plus named promoted runtime gates.

compare.title: Token Efficiency Matters
compare.subtitle: AI models pay per token. Vais uses 33% fewer tokens than Rust and 40% fewer than C for equivalent programs — single-char keywords, expression bodies, and auto-return add up across codebases.
compare.barNote: fibonacci + quicksort + http_types + linked_list (tiktoken cl100k_base). See benchmarks.

performance.title: Native Performance
performance.subtitle: Vais compiles to optimized LLVM IR — on par with C and Rust at runtime, with fast compilation.
performance.compileNote: Vais --emit-ir vs full compilation. 8.6x faster than C, 19x faster than Rust. Updated 2026-02-11.

features.title: Why Vais?
features.subtitle: Designed from the ground up for AI-assisted development without sacrificing performance.

singleChar.title: Single-Char Keywords
singleChar.description: F for function, S for struct, I/E for if/else, L for loop, M for match. Minimal tokens, maximum clarity.

selfRecursion.title: Self-Recursion Operator
selfRecursion.description: The @ operator calls the current function recursively. No need to repeat the function name — AI-friendly and concise.

expression.title: Everything is an Expression
expression.description: If/else, match, and blocks all return values. No return keyword needed for the last expression. Functional and ergonomic.

llvm.title: LLVM Native Speed
llvm.description: Compiles promoted native paths through LLVM. Broader optimization and backend claims are tracked by explicit certification gates.

inference.title: Type Inference
inference.description: Bidirectional type checking infers types automatically. Write x := 42 instead of explicit type annotations everywhere.

toolchain.title: Tooling Workbench
toolchain.description: LSP server, formatter, debugger, REPL, package manager, and IDE plugins exist as implementation surfaces. Support level depends on named gates.

multiTarget.title: Native First, Experimental Extra Targets
multiTarget.description: The promoted path is native codegen. JavaScript, WebAssembly, and browser execution paths remain experimental unless a page names a gate.

incremental.title: Parallel Compiler Pipeline
incremental.description: Per-module dependency tracking and parallel codegen are active workbench surfaces; exact performance claims require benchmark gates.

selfHosting.title: Self-Hosting Workbench
selfHosting.description: The repository contains 50,000+ lines of Vais compiler sources used for bootstrap and conformance work. Current guarantees are the certified Core and promoted runtime gates.

playground.title: Try Vais Now
playground.subtitle: Try Vais syntax and examples in the browser. Server-backed compilation is used when available; browser-only JS/WASM paths are experimental.

install.title: Build From Source or Try the Playground
install.oneClickNote: Detects your OS & arch automatically. macOS, Linux, WSL.

footer.copyright: © 2026 Vais Contributors. Built with ❤ for AI.

vaisx.title: Build Web Apps with Vais
vaisx.subtitle: VaisX is the Vais web framework workbench. Current public claims are tied to promoted runtime, unit, package, and full-build gates.

compiler.title: Compile-Time Reactivity
compiler.description: Reactive state analysis and DOM update generation are active implementation surfaces verified only where named gates cover them.

routing.title: File-Based Routing & SSR
routing.description: File-based routing and SSR/SSG modules exist; promoted behavior is documented by the current Vais Web gates.

runtime.title: Promoted Runtime Gates
runtime.description: The current Vais Web baseline is runtime smoke 61/77, unit 390/390, package tests 3272/3272, and full-build 24/24. Size claims require a size gate.
